title: Assessment of Physiotherapy Practice-Chinese to Evaluate Student Performance
author: Hu Jia
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.598g99w

steps.title: Two physiotherapy academic staff from the...
steps.instruction: Two physiotherapy academic staff from the involved university who received their education in China and English-speaking countries translated the English APP into Mandarin Chinese with Simplified Chinese characters (Chinese version 1).

steps.title: This version was then reviewed by...
steps.instruction: This version was then reviewed by a third bilingual physiotherapy educator (AJ), with teaching experience in both China and Australia, as well as research experience in education.

steps.title: Any discrepancies or minor modifications to...
steps.instruction: Any discrepancies or minor modifications to the wordings and terminology proposed by educator AJ were discussed by the 3 educators and an agreed Chinese version-2 was developed.

steps.title: An experienced physiotherapy academic fluent in...
steps.instruction: An experienced physiotherapy academic fluent in both English and Chinese languages and with publishing experience in both languages was invited to translate the Chinese version-2 back into English. This educator was blinded to the English version of the APP and Chinese version 1 prior to her participation in this project. The outcome of this exercise was an English Version 3.

steps.title: Version 3 was then sent to...
steps.instruction: Version 3 was then sent to the original author of APP for comparison with the original English version.

steps.title: A skype meeting was then held...
steps.instruction: A skype meeting was then held between AJ and the original author of APP, to discuss the significance of any wording differences between the two English versions.

steps.title: A final meeting was then held...
steps.instruction: A final meeting was then held with all 5 educators for ratification of any modifications deemed necessary.

steps.title: The final version (APP-Chinese) (Appendix II)...
steps.instruction: The final version (APP-Chinese) (Appendix II) was produced to be trialed by students and clinical educators in the involved university.

steps.title: CEs from the involved university were...
steps.instruction: CEs from the involved university were invited to attend a workshop to introduce the APP-Chinese and were provided with information on scoring rules associated with the APP-Chinese assessment form and examples of behavioral performance indicators extracted and translated from the APP-ANZ manual.

steps.title: Preparation of the students - 4...
steps.instruction: Preparation of the students - 4 th year physiotherapy students at the involved university were introduced to the APP-Chinese and the associated performance/ behaviour indicators were explained.

steps.title: Video vignettes of varying levels of...
steps.instruction: Video vignettes of varying levels of student performance covering inadequate, adequate and excellent standards were used to facilitate calibration of assessor expectations and achieve consensus moderation.

steps.title: A mid-unit feedback was organised for...
steps.instruction: A mid-unit feedback was organised for each student during the 4th week of each placement.

steps.title: An end-unit feedback was organised at...
steps.instruction: An end-unit feedback was organised at the end of 8th week of each placement.

steps.title: After three rounds of clinical placements,...
steps.instruction: After three rounds of clinical placements, students and CEs were asked to complete the respective feedback questionnaires on the use of the APP-Chinese. These questionnaires were translated from the English APP-ANZ feedback questionnaire used during the development and evaluation of the APP-ANZ.

steps.title: To explore views on the use...
steps.instruction: To explore views on the use of the APP-Chinese that might not be reflected by the standard APP questionnaire, a semi-structured focus group discussion session was separately organised for students and CEs after completion of the feedback questionnaires.

steps.title: The group meeting focused on the following
steps.instruction: The group meeting focused on the following: comment on the 20-items in APP-Chinese; comments on the ease of understanding the wordings used in the APP-Chinese; applicability of the suggested examples of performance indicators for the Chinese clinical education environment; and any further comment regarding this method of assessment of clinical placement performance (using the APP-Chinese).
